rationale: Located in Humble, TX (Houston metro), a Southern climate favoring 4:12–6:12 pitches. Roof shadows extend approximately 10–18 ft from the eave edge, consistent with a moderate pitch for a single-story eave height of ~8–9 ft. Viewed from nadir, the roof planes are broad but the ridge is clearly recessed from the eave footprint, ruling out very shallow (3:12–4:12) or steep (8:12+) pitches. The dominant hip-roof configuration with dark asphalt shingles and no pronounced dormer protrusions is characteristic of 1990s–2000s Texas suburban tract construction, where 6:12 is the most common standard pitch. Shadow definition on the shingle surface is moderate, further supporting a 6:12 estimate.
